body:
`conditional_length` has a lot of specializations for `NTuple` that create ambiguities, also for an immersed field we have a generic unspecialized `dims` method.

This PR cleans up a bit the `conditional_length` method, generalizing the `Tuple` behavior and avoiding ambiguities.
title: Clear ambiguities in `conditional_length`

body: Repeat of #4671.  This was originally introduced in https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/pull/3880#issuecomment-2462033959, but I don't understand the motivation very well, might just be a problem which happened back then and has been resolved since.  I removed this dependency in #5458, and tests still passed (all tests also passed in #4671).  Close #5465

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

title: Remove dependency on `CUDA_Runtime_jll` in `test/` environment

body:
Addresses item B3 from #5349.

## Summary

Adds a `## Bulk drag boundary conditions` section to `docs/src/models/boundary_conditions.md` covering:

- **Formulations**: `QuadraticFormulation` (standard ``C^D |U| u``) vs `LinearFormulation` (Rayleigh friction ``C^D u``), with units clarified for each
- **Domain bottom drag**: Basic usage with `FieldBoundaryConditions(bottom=drag)`, showing that the velocity direction is auto-inferred
- **Immersed boundary drag**: Using `ImmersedBoundaryCondition(bottom=drag)` to target specific facets, and the shorthand `immersed=drag` for all non-normal facets
- **Background velocities**: The `background_velocities=(U∞, V∞, W∞)` keyword for simulations with a mean current (as used in the tilted bottom boundary layer example)

All examples are `jldoctest` blocks drawing from the existing docstrings in `src/Models/BulkDragBoundaryConditions.jl`.

## Test plan

- [x] Docs build without errors (`julia --project=docs/ docs/make.jl`)
- [x] All new jldoctest blocks pass
title: Add BulkDrag boundary conditions documentation

body:
An attempt to address items B7, C1, C5, C6 from #5349.

## Summary

- **C1 — Units** (`quick_start.md`): New `## Units` section listing all `Oceananigans.Units` constants with a table and example. This module is used in 8 of 14 examples but was never explained anywhere.
- **C5 — `SphericalCoriolis`** (`models/coriolis.md`): Expanded the Spherical Coriolis section to explain the difference between `HydrostaticSphericalCoriolis` (traditional approximation) and `SphericalCoriolis` (includes non-traditional horizontal terms), with a constructor example for each.
- **B7 — `background_closure_fluxes`** (`models/background_fields.md`): New section explaining that turbulent closure fluxes from background field gradients are off by default and showing how to enable them with `BackgroundFields(background_closure_fluxes=true, ...)`.
- **C6 — `PiecewiseLinearMask`** (`models/forcing_functions.md`): New `### Mask types` subsection introducing `GaussianMask` and `PiecewiseLinearMask` side-by-side with a jldoctest and guidance on when to use each. Clarified that the default `mask` equals 1 everywhere so the forcing applies over the full domain.

## Test plan

- [ ] Docs build without errors (`julia --project=docs/ docs/make.jl`)
- [ ] All new jldoctest blocks pass
title: Fill small documentation gaps (Units, SphericalCoriolis, background_closure_fluxes, PiecewiseLinearMask)
